description:
You are an MLOps engineer working inside a Git repository located at

    /home/user/experiments/model-training

Current repository state:
 • A single file: /home/user/experiments/model-training/README.md  
   └─ Contents (one line, ending with a newline):  
      Model training experiments

 • Branch name: main  
 • There are NO Git tags yet.  
 • HEAD already points to the latest (and only) commit that tracks README.md.

Goal:
1. Create a lightweight tag named exactly
      experiment_ready
   that points to the current HEAD.

2. Immediately after creating the tag, capture the list of all tags in the
   repository using the command
      git tag --list
   and save that exact output to the file

      /home/user/experiments/model-training/git_tags.log

Required formatting for git_tags.log:
 • It must contain precisely one line:
      experiment_ready
 • The line must end with a single trailing newline character (Unix LF).
 • No extra spaces, blank lines, or other characters are allowed.

Hints & constraints:
 • Achieve the goal with a single Git command for tagging (complexity: simple single terminal command).  
 • You may use additional commands only for verification/log-generation purposes (e.g., redirecting the output of git tag --list into the log file).
 • Do not modify README.md or create any other files.
 • The automated test will:
     1. Check that a lightweight tag named experiment_ready exists and points to the current HEAD commit.
     2. Confirm that /home/user/experiments/model-training/git_tags.log exists, is a regular file, and its contents match the exact specification above.
truth:
Initial repository layout before the agent starts:
  Directory: /home/user/experiments/model-training
    ├── .git/                     (standard Git directory structure)
    └── README.md                 (tracked, committed)

  README.md content (including newline):
    "Model training experiments\n"

  Branch: main
  Tag list: (empty)

Expected final state after successful task completion:
  1. Git tag list contains exactly one tag:
         experiment_ready
     It is a lightweight tag (i.e., appears in refs/tags/experiment_ready and not refs/tags/experiment_ready^{}) and its object hash equals HEAD's commit hash.

     Verification command used by test:
         git -C /home/user/experiments/model-training rev-parse HEAD
         git -C /home/user/experiments/model-training rev-parse refs/tags/experiment_ready
     The two hashes must be identical.

  2. File /home/user/experiments/model-training/git_tags.log exists with mode 0644 (agent default umask is acceptable) and content:
         "experiment_ready\n"

  No other changes (no extra tags, branches, commits, or files).
